pattern: GET route returns a resource without checking requester owns it
bad_example:
note = db.query(Note).get(note_id)
return note  # any logged-in user can read any note
fix: scope every read query by the authenticated user's ownership or admin role
source: Internship Week 3 — cross-user read access gap

pattern: filter matches substrings instead of exact or tokenized values
fix: match whole tokens (split tags, exact equality, or regex word boundaries), never raw substring contains on delimited fields

pattern: logout invalidates the session after other concurrent requests have already passed auth checks
fix: rotate or revoke session tokens server-side immediately, reject requests using the old token, and avoid relying on client-side cookie clear alone

pattern: authorization check uses a client-supplied user id instead of the authenticated identity
fix: derive the subject from the session or JWT claims; never trust a user_id from query params or body for ownership decisions
source: Personal review checklist — IDOR via trusted request params

pattern: error responses or debug logs leak internal identifiers, stack traces, or neighboring users' data
bad_example:
except Exception as e:
    return jsonify({'error': str(e), 'query': sql})
fix: return generic client errors, log details server-side only, and never include other users' records in error payloads
source: Personal review checklist — over-verbose error handling
